commitResults.messageBody: Make UI improvements (add labels like "Email: ", minor tweaks)
Relax room unit to allow 1-3 digits
Make name and email case sensitive
Add error message for duplicate indices in delete command

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix PED bug-report mismatches in help, usage, and email validation.
commitResults.messageBody:
This aligns in-app guidance with the User Guide by linking directly to UserGuide.html and documenting edit's -newtag flag, and it updates email parsing to allow consecutive local-part special characters as described.

Made-with: Cursor

commitResults.messageTitle: Use newlines instead of semicolons when displaying details
Fix bug where filtered list resets after comment
Treat blank json file as not present
Add chain of command to pass down startup message
Add missing headings to site map

commitResults.messageBody: Fix duplicate handling for 12-02 and 12-2
Fix PED bug-report mismatches in help, usage, and email validation.

commitResults.messageTitle: Clarify tag addition process in documentation

commitResults.messageBody: Update DG based on PED bugs
Fix bug where filtered list resets after comment

commitResults.messageTitle: Clarify invalid delete index comma formats in UG
commitResults.messageBody: Document that delete indices must not contain trailing, leading, or consecutive commas. This clarifies that inputs like "delete 1," are invalid command format.

commitResults.messageTitle: Clarify in UG that find does not search comments
commitResults.messageBody: Update the find command documentation to explicitly state its search scope is limited to name, room, and tags. Clarifies expected behavior and avoids confusion when comments are not matched.

commitResults.messageTitle: Clarify reserved prefix tokens in UG input text
commitResults.messageBody: Add a command-format note that prefix-like tokens such as p/, c/, n/, e/, r/, and t/ are reserved by the parser and may be interpreted as new fields. This clarifies expected input behavior and avoids ambiguous free-text usage.

commitResults.messageTitle: Standardise UG terminology with RACE-first wording
commitResults.messageBody:
Refine UG terminology to consistently present RACE as the product name
while using "address book" naturally as a functional description where
appropriate.

Clarify this framing in the introduction, keep technical references like
addressbook.jar explicit, and smooth related wording in quick start/data
sections for consistency.

commitResults.messageTitle: Enhance UG with command distributed visuals
commitResults.messageBody:
Embed command-focused screenshots across the User Guide to improve coverage of key workflows, including add/edit/comment/find/list/delete outcomes and error feedback.

Also refine terminology and command-format clarifications for
consistency (RACE-first wording, find scope, reserved prefixes, and
delete comma-input guidance).

commitResults.messageTitle: Refine the list command format line to a single unambiguous sentence to avoid duplicate-reading confusion.
Add FAQ backup link to delete and clear tips in UG
commitResults.messageBody:
Improve backup guidance by linking delete/clear tips directly to the
Saving and data FAQ section. This makes it easier for users to find
where data/addressbook.json is located and how to copy it before
destructive actions.

commitResults.messageTitle: Clarify -newtag behavior and link to FAQ in UG
commitResults.messageBody: Document that using -newtag without any t/ tag may still succeed but does not create a tag. Add direct links to FAQ rules/limitations from add and tag guidance, andinclude a dedicated FAQ entry for this scenario.

commitResults.messageTitle: Improve readability in the User Guide by presenting the two possible list command outputs as bullet points under Expected Output. This removes ambiguity from the previous line-by-line formatting.
Clarify single-field sorting constraint in UG
commitResults.messageBody: Make it explicit in the list command docs that only one sort field is allowed per command. Add an invalid multi-field example (list -sort n/ r/) in both note and caution sections to reduce ambiguity.

Clarify that room uniqueness is intentional in this app’s Residential College's context by stating the one-resident-per-room assumption in the introduction andadd-command notes. This explains why duplicate room numbers are treated as invalid without changing behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: Update the README resident field list to match the fields actually supported by the app and documented in the User Guide. Replace outdated entries with current fields: Name, Room, Phone number, Email, Tags, and Comment.
Polish wording in assumptions and add-command notes to use 'Residential College (RC) context' consistently.

commitResults.messageBody:
Adding a person resets the list to show all residents, but the active
sort order is cleared as a side effect.

Clearing the sort order makes sorted workflows less predictable. After
`list -sort ...`, a newly added resident appears at the end instead of
the correct sorted position.

Let's

*Keep the existing comparator when adding a person so sorting remains stable while still showing all residents.

*Add a regression test to confirm that adding a person preserves the
active sort order and inserts the new entry in the expected position.

commitResults.messageTitle: Refine UG & DG to reflect sort retaining after using add command
